evals.prompt:
You are interpreting lab test results for a Uganda road project: the Gulu-Atiak trunk road upgrade (UNRA). Five soil samples from subgrade test pits along a 10 km section have been tested. The region has high rainfall (>1200 mm/year) with a distinct wet season.

Sample results:
| Sample | Chainage | Depth (m) | CBR (soaked 4-day, 100% MDD) | LL (%) | PL (%) | PI (%) | LS (%) | MDD T-99 (kg/m3) | OMC (%) |
|--------|----------|-----------|------|--------|--------|--------|--------|---------|--------|
| TP-01 | 0+500 | 0.5-1.0 | 12 | 38 | 19 | 19 | 8.0 | 1780 | 16.2 |
| TP-02 | 2+500 | 0.5-1.0 | 18 | 32 | 17 | 15 | 6.2 | 1850 | 14.5 |
| TP-03 | 4+500 | 0.5-1.0 | 8 | 45 | 22 | 23 | 9.5 | 1720 | 18.0 |
| TP-04 | 6+500 | 0.5-1.0 | 15 | 35 | 18 | 17 | 7.0 | 1810 | 15.3 |
| TP-05 | 9+000 | 0.5-1.0 | 22 | 28 | 16 | 12 | 5.0 | 1900 | 13.0 |

Additionally, a borrow pit investigation found laterite gravel with the following results:
| Sample | CBR (soaked 4-day, 95% mod AASHTO) | LL (%) | PL (%) | PI (%) | MDD T-180 (kg/m3) | OMC (%) |
|--------|------|--------|--------|--------|---------|--------|
| BP-A | 42 | 30 | 18 | 12 | 2020 | 10.5 |
| BP-B | 35 | 33 | 19 | 14 | 1980 | 11.2 |
| BP-C | 48 | 27 | 17 | 10 | 2050 | 9.8 |

Interpret all results. Assess subgrade classification per MoWT Table 3.1. Assess borrow pit material suitability for subbase per MoWT Table 5.1. Identify any concerns and provide recommendations.
evals.expected_output: Subgrade CBR values range from 8-22%, giving subgrade classes S3 (TP-03 at 8%), S4 (TP-01 at 12%, TP-04 at 15%), and S5 (TP-02 at 18%, TP-05 at 22%). TP-03 is the weakest section. PI values of 12-23% indicate moderate to high plasticity subgrade soils. Borrow pit material: All 3 samples meet CBR >= 30% for subbase (Table 5.1). BP-C (PI=10) meets PI < 12 recommendation; BP-A (PI=12) is borderline; BP-B (PI=14) exceeds PI limit. Overall, borrow pit is suitable for subbase but BP-B may need PI reduction (lime treatment). Material is NOT suitable for base (CBR < 80%).

assertions.text: Correctly classifies subgrade per MoWT Table 3.1: TP-03 (CBR 8%) = S4 (8-14%), TP-01 (CBR 12%) = S4, TP-04 (CBR 15%) = S5 (15-29%), TP-02 (CBR 18%) = S5, TP-05 (CBR 22%) = S5
Correctly assesses all borrow pit samples against subbase CBR >= 30% criterion from Table 5.1 and confirms all pass
Identifies PI compliance issue: flags that BP-B (PI=14) exceeds the recommended PI < 12 limit for subbase materials and recommends treatment (lime, screening, or blending)
States that borrow pit material is NOT suitable for base course (CBR well below 80% requirement in Table 5.1)
Notes that soaked CBR is appropriate for this region given rainfall > 1200 mm/year (wet conditions per MoWT Table 3.2)

evals.prompt:
You are interpreting lab test results for a UK road improvement project: a rural road in North Yorkshire. The subgrade is glacial till (boulder clay). Three trial pit samples have been tested per BS 1377.

Subgrade results:
| Sample | Equilibrium CBR (%) | LL (%) | PL (%) | PI (%) | MCV | Description |
|--------|-----|--------|--------|--------|-----|-------------|
| TP-A | 3.5 | 52 | 23 | 29 | 8 | Firm to stiff brown boulder clay |
| TP-B | 2.8 | 58 | 25 | 33 | 6 | Stiff grey boulder clay with gravel |
| TP-C | 4.2 | 46 | 21 | 25 | 9 | Firm brown clay with sand and gravel |

A proposed Type 1 sub-base aggregate has been tested:
| Property | Result | SHW Requirement |
|----------|--------|-----------------|
| 10% fines value (dry) | 62 kN | > 50 kN |
| 10% fines value (soaked) | 52 kN | > 75% of dry |
| Passing 37.5 mm | 100% | 100% |
| Passing 20 mm | 68% | 55-100% |
| Passing 10 mm | 42% | 25-85% |
| Passing 5 mm | 28% | 10-65% |
| Passing 0.6 mm | 10% | 0-25% |
| Passing 0.075 mm | 5% | 0-10% |

Interpret the results. Determine the foundation class per CD 225. Assess the Type 1 sub-base material against SHW requirements.
evals.expected_output: Subgrade is Foundation Class 2 (CBR 2-5%). Design CBR approximately 3% (lower bound). Capping layer required per CD 225. Boulder clay has high PI (25-33%) but is generally not frost-susceptible due to low permeability. Type 1 sub-base: 10% fines value passes (62 kN > 50 kN, soaked/dry ratio 52/62 = 84% > 75%). Grading within SHW Table 8/3 limits. Material acceptable as Type 1 sub-base.

assertions.text: Assigns Foundation Class 2 (CBR 2-5% range) based on the equilibrium CBR values of 2.8-4.2%
Recommends capping layer requirement for Foundation Class 2 subgrade per CD 225
Confirms Type 1 sub-base passes 10% fines value test (62 kN > 50 kN minimum) and soaked/dry ratio meets requirements
Confirms Type 1 sub-base grading is within SHW specification limits for all sieve sizes

evals.prompt:
You are interpreting lab test results for a Uganda road project. The Engineer has provided the following test results for a borrow pit material being considered for base course:

| Sample | CBR (unsoaked, 98% mod AASHTO) | LL (%) | PL (%) | PI (%) | MDD T-180 (kg/m3) | OMC (%) |
|--------|------|--------|--------|--------|---------|--------|
| S1 | 95 | 35 | 18 | 17 | 1950 | 12.5 |
| S2 | 88 | 38 | 19 | 19 | 1920 | 13.2 |
| S3 | 72 | 32 | 17 | 15 | 1980 | 11.8 |

The project is located in western Uganda with annual rainfall exceeding 1500 mm and a high water table. The contractor insists the material meets MoWT Table 5.1 requirements for granular base. Interpret the results and assess suitability.
evals.expected_output: This is a trap scenario with multiple issues. (1) CBR was tested unsoaked -- for a wet region (>1500 mm rainfall), soaked CBR is required per MoWT Table 3.2. The actual soaked CBR is likely to be significantly lower. (2) PI of 15-19 far exceeds the PI < 6 limit for base course (MoWT Appendix A). (3) Only S1 and S2 have CBR > 80% -- but these are unsoaked values. (4) High CBR with high PI is an inconsistency flag -- the material may perform well dry but will degrade rapidly when wet. Conclusion: Material does NOT meet MoWT base specifications. Flag both the wrong test method and the excessive PI. Recommend re-testing with soaked CBR and/or lime treatment to reduce PI.

assertions.text: Flags that unsoaked CBR is inappropriate for this wet region (rainfall > 1500 mm) and that soaked CBR is required per MoWT Table 3.2
Flags that PI of 15-19 exceeds the PI < 6 specification limit for granular base material per MoWT Appendix A
Identifies the inconsistency between high CBR and high PI as a concern -- material is moisture-sensitive and soaked CBR will likely be much lower
Concludes the material does NOT meet MoWT base specifications and recommends re-testing with soaked CBR and/or treatment to reduce PI
Raises an escalation flag for the use of unsoaked CBR in a wet region and for the excessive PI

evals.prompt: Interpret the following test results for a Kenya road project base material: CBR=82, PI=8, LL=28, MDD=2.15 t/m3, OMC=8.2%. The material is being assessed for use as a granular base on a Class A road. Determine compliance with Kenya material specifications and identify any missing test data.
evals.expected_output: Assess against RDM 3.3 Table 8.8 material codes and Chart GM10 (G80). CBR 82% >= 80% minimum for G80 = PASS. PI 8 <= Max 10 for G80 = PASS. But LAA and ACV data are missing -- G80 requires LAA Max 50% and ACV Max 35%. Grading data also needed to check envelope compliance and Plasticity Modulus (Max 250). KS/EN test standards should be referenced (BS EN ISO 17892-12 for Atterberg limits). Kenya is transitioning from BS to KS/EN standards per RDM 3.2.

assertions.text: References KS/EN test standards or notes the transition from BS to KS/EN standards in Kenya per RDM 3.2
Assesses CBR against G80 requirement (minimum 80%) from RDM 3.3 and confirms it passes
Identifies missing test data (LAA, ACV, or grading) needed for complete G80 compliance assessment per RDM 3.3 Chart GM10
References Kenya material specification system (G80, Table 8.8, or Chart GM10) rather than Uganda G-code system

evals.prompt: A DCP test on an ERA trunk road project in Ethiopia gives a penetration rate of 15 mm/blow at a depth of 300-600 mm below the existing surface. Using the ERA Flexible Pavement Design Manual Vol I 2013 Appendix I, convert this to an estimated CBR value and determine the subgrade class. Also specify what laboratory confirmation tests ERA requires.
evals.expected_output: Per ERA Pavement Vol I Appendix I: DCP penetration rate of 15 mm/blow corresponds to an estimated CBR of approximately 8-12% using the DCP-CBR correlation. This places the subgrade in class S3 (CBR 8-14%) per ERA Table 3-1. Laboratory confirmation requires soaked CBR test per AASHTO T-193, compacted at Standard Proctor (AASHTO T-99) for subgrade. ERA's DCP correlation is provided in Appendix I. Five subgrade classes S1-S5 (not six as in Uganda/Kenya).

assertions.text: References ERA Pavement Vol I 2013 Appendix I for DCP-CBR correlation (not Uganda or Kenya)
Converts 15 mm/blow DCP to estimated CBR in the 8-12% range
Classifies subgrade as S3 (CBR 8-14%) using ERA's five-class system (S1-S5)
Specifies AASHTO T-193 (CBR) and AASHTO T-99 (Standard Proctor) as ERA-required test standards
